perturbation.original_text: 2.  HANDLING OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1; (b) not to copy, reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the Discloser; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third (3rd) parties except in confidence to such of its directors, employees, servants, agents, officers, representatives, consultants,
perturbation.changed_text: 2.  HANDLING OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; (b) not to copy, reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the Discloser; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third (3rd) parties except in confidence to such of its directors, employees, servants, agents, officers, representatives, consultants,
perturbation.explanation: By removing sub-clause (iii) in clause 2.1(a), which restricts using confidential information to search publicly available information, it directly contradicts the intent of maintaining the confidentiality of proprietary know-how. This omission creates an in-text contradiction as the general intent is still to protect the confidential information, but one specific crucial means of protection has been removed. This is a contradiction because it removes a key protection of the confidential information. This omission implies the confidential information can be used for guidance of search of public information, contradicting other sections of this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; (b) not to copy, reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies, reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the Discloser; (c) not to disclose the same whether to its employees or to third (3rd) parties except in confidence to such of its directors, employees, servants, agents, officers, representatives, consultants,
perturbation.justification: The change removes sub-clause (iii) from section 2.1(a), which previously restricted the use of confidential information to guide searches of publicly available information. The contradicted text, which is the original text, includes this restriction. By omitting this sub-clause, the changed text implies that the confidential information *can* be used for searching public information, directly contradicting the original intent to maintain confidentiality as outlined in the contradicted section. This creates an in-text contradiction by removing a key protective measure.

perturbation.original_text: 2.2 The Company shall cause its directors, agents, employees, servants, officers, representatives, consultants, independent contractors and professional advisors involved in the Purpose to observe or be similarly bound by the t erms of Agreement. The Company as the principal party shall be responsible and held liable for any breach of this Agreement by any of its directors, agents, employees, servants, officers, representatives, consultants, independent contractors and profession al advisors.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 The Company shall cause its directors, agents, employees, servants, officers, representatives, consultants, independent contractors and professional advisors involved in the Purpose to observe or be similarly bound by the t erms of Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second sentence of clause 2.2, the Company's liability for breaches by its personnel is eliminated. This omission creates an in-text contradiction with the overall intent of the NDA, which is to protect AHPL's confidential information. Now, there's no clear recourse if an employee of the company breaches the NDA, even though the confidentiality obligations still exist. The text creates contradiction because the first sentence of this section tells the company must ensure their personnel comply with the NDA, but the second sentence removes the liability for when their personnel DO NOT comply with the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The Company shall cause its directors, agents, employees, servants, officers, representatives, consultants, independent contractors and professional advisors involved in the Purpose to observe or be similarly bound by the t erms of Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Company is liable for breaches of the agreement by its personnel. The changed text removes this liability, directly contradicting the original intent and the contradicted text, which also describes the company's personnel being bound by the agreement. The removal of the liability clause creates a strong in-text contradiction because it implies that while personnel must observe the agreement, the company is not responsible if they don't, making enforcement unclear and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Each party shall: (a) within one (1) month of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 6 NDA_ 01012014 other party that it has complied with the requiremen ts of this sub - clause; and (b) notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Each party shall: (a) within one (1) month of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 6 NDA_ 01012014 other party that it has complied with the requiremen ts of this sub - clause;
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause 3(b), the obligation to continue being bound by the undertakings in Clause 2 after returning the confidential information is removed. This creates an in-text contradiction because while the documents are returned, the confidential information can potentially be used in ways violating Clause 2, after the returns. This is a contradiction because the expectation of the NDA is for the information to be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Each party shall: (a) within one (1) month of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 6 NDA_ 01012014 other party that it has complied with the requiremen ts of this sub - clause;
perturbation.justification: The original text included clause 3(b) which ensured that the obligations in Clause 2 (confidentiality undertakings) remained in effect even after the confidential information was returned. The changed text removes clause 3(b). This directly contradicts the intention of the NDA because after the return of the documents, the party is no longer explicitly bound by Clause 2. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text (likely a formatting error), but the key is that clause 3(b) is missing from the changed text, thus making it so that Clause 2 doesn't necessarily have to be followed after the documents are returned, even though the expectation of the NDA is that the information is to be protected.

perturbation.original_text: 15.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement or the existence, validity, termination, application or interpretation of this Agreement or any of its provisions, both parties shall use their best endeavours to settle the dispute informally by agreement between the parties. Both parties shall always act in good faith and co -operate with each other to resolve any disputes. 15.2 Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, if the dispute is not settled in accordance with Clause 14.1 above, no party shall pr oceed to litigation or any other form of dispute resolution unless the parties have made reasonable efforts to resolve the same through mediation in accordance with the mediation rules of the Singapore Mediation Centre. A party who receives a notice for me diation from the other party shall consent and participate in the mediation process in accordance with this clause. Failure to comply with this clause shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.1 In the event of any dispute or difference arising out of or in connection with or in relation to this Agreement or the existence, validity, termination, application or interpretation of this Agreement or any of its provisions, both parties shall use their best endeavours to settle the dispute informally by agreement between the parties. Both parties shall always act in good faith and co -operate with each other to resolve any disputes. 15.2 Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, if the dispute is not settled in accordance with Clause 15.1 above, no party shall pr oceed to litigation or any other form of dispute resolution unless the parties have made reasonable efforts to resolve the same through mediation in accordance with the mediation rules of the Singapore Mediation Centre. A party who receives a notice for me diation from the other party shall consent and participate in the mediation process in accordance with this clause. Failure to comply with this clause shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the reference from 'Clause 14.1' to 'Clause 15.1' in Clause 15.2, a contradiction is created. Clause 14.1 relates to 'Waiver', not Dispute Resolution. Therefore, stating that if the dispute is not settled as described in the waiver clause, the parties must mediate creates an in-text contradiction, as the waiver clause doesn't have anything to do with dispute resolution. It now incorrectly connects the process to a waiver of rights, creating uncertainty and conflicting meaning. Now Clause 15.2 is referencing a action, that contradicts the intent of Clause 15. This is a contradiction because it incorrectly connects the process to a waiver of rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.2 Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, if the dispute is not settled in accordance with Clause 15.1 above, no party shall pr oceed to litigation or any other form of dispute resolution unless the parties have made reasonable efforts to resolve the same through mediation in accordance with the mediation rules of the Singapore Mediation Centre. A party who receives a notice for me diation from the other party shall consent and participate in the mediation process in accordance with this clause. Failure to comply with this clause shall be deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text referenced Clause 14.1 (Waiver) appropriately. The changed text incorrectly references Clause 15.1 within Clause 15.2. Clause 15.2 requires mediation if a dispute is not settled as per Clause 15.1. However, Clause 15.1 describes an informal settlement, not a formal dispute resolution process which means the parties have to mediate even if they settle the matter informally, therefore creating a contradiction to the contradicted text which makes the mediation clause unclear and contradictory as it now relates to informal settlement and not a separate dispute resolution process. This creates a strong contradiction as it alters the fundamental intent of the dispute resolution process making it unclear when mediation is actually required, in contrast to the original clear intention.
